name: Blob Track TOP
description: The Blob Track TOP is implemented using OpenCV.
summary: The Blob Track TOP is implemented using OpenCV.

parameters.name: Reset
parameters.label: Reset
parameters.description: Resets all tracking data and learned background data while this parameter is On.

parameters.name: Reset Pulse
parameters.label: Reset Pulse
parameters.description: resetpulse - Instantly resets all tracking data and learned background data.

parameters.name: Mono Source
parameters.label: Mono Source
parameters.description:
monosource -  - Blob tracking is done using a single channel. This menu controls what single channel is used to detect blobs.

 luminance - red - green - blue - alpha - rgbaverage - average -

parameters.name: Luminance
parameters.label: Luminance
parameters.description: luminance - red - green - blue - alpha - rgbaverage - average -

parameters.name: Draw Blob Bounds
parameters.label: Draw Blob Bounds
parameters.description: drawblobs - Draws rectangles on the  image that shows where the tracked blobs are.

parameters.name: Blob Bound Color
parameters.label: Blob Bound Color
parameters.description:
blobcolor -  - Determines the color of the rectangles that are drawn to show the blobs.

 blobcolorr - blobcolorg - blobcolorb -

parameters.name: Red
parameters.label: Red
parameters.description: blobcolorr - blobcolorg - blobcolorb -

parameters.name: Threshold
parameters.label: Threshold
parameters.description: threshold - Threshold used to create the binary texture when using background subtraction. It is the threshold of the difference between the background texture and the input texture.

parameters.name: Minimum Blob Size
parameters.label: Minimum Blob Size
parameters.description: minblobsize - Blobs must be at least this big to be tracked.

parameters.name: Maximum Blob Size
parameters.label: Maximum Blob Size
parameters.description: maxblobsize - Blobs larger than this will not be tracked.

parameters.name: Maximum Move Distance
parameters.label: Maximum Move Distance
parameters.description: maxmovedistance - The maximum distance a blob can move in one frame and still be considered to the same blob (maintain the same ID).

parameters.name: Delete Nearby Blobs
parameters.label: Delete Nearby Blobs
parameters.description: deletenearby - Sometimes (depending on the tracking method) duplicate blobs may be created. This feature allows you to delete blobs that are too close to each other.

parameters.name: Minimum Distance
parameters.label: Minimum Distance
parameters.description: deletedist - When deleting nearby blobs, blobs will be deleted if they are within this number of pixels of each other. The smaller blob will be deleted.

parameters.name: Delete Area Tolerance
parameters.label: Delete Area Tolerance
parameters.description: deletenearbytol - Along with the distance, the area of the two blobs can be compared. If this parameter is 1, than the area is ignored. As this parameter gets smaller only blobs that have a significant size difference (between the two blobs being compared) will be deleted.

parameters.name: Delete Overlapping Blobs
parameters.label: Delete Overlapping Blobs
parameters.description: deleteoverlap - Deletes blobs that are overlapping.

parameters.name: Delete Overlap Tolerance
parameters.label: Delete Overlap Tolerance
parameters.description: deleteoverlaptol - If this parameter is 1 then only blobs that are completely overlapped will be deleted. As this value gets smaller less and less overlap is needed for a blob to get deleted.

parameters.name: Revive Blobs
parameters.label: Revive Blobs
parameters.description: reviveblobs - When enabled, will revive lost blobs (ie. same ID) if they satisfy all the below parameters

parameters.name: Revive Time
parameters.label: Revive Time
parameters.description: revivetime - The time (in seconds) threshold for reviving a lost blob. If a blob has been lost for longer than revive time, it will not be revived and is considered expired.

parameters.name: Revive Area Difference
parameters.label: Revive Area Difference
parameters.description: revivearea - The area difference threshold for the new blob and the lost blob.

parameters.name: Revive Distance
parameters.label: Revive Distance
parameters.description: revivedistance - The distance threshold between the new blob and the lost blob.

parameters.name: Include Lost Blobs in Table
parameters.label: Include Lost Blobs in Table
parameters.description: includelost - When enabled, lost blobs will be included in the Blob Track 's Info  table.

parameters.name: Include Expired Blobs in Table
parameters.label: Include Expired Blobs in Table
parameters.description: includeexpired - When enabled, expired blobs (ie. blobs that have no chance of revival) will be included in the Blob Track 's Info  table.

parameters.name: Expired Time
parameters.label: Expired Time
parameters.description: expiredtime - Time in seconds for blobs to remain in the Info  table after expiring.

parameters.name: Output Resolution
parameters.label: Output Resolution
parameters.description:
outputresolution -  - quickly change the resolution of the 's data.

 useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Use Input
parameters.label: Use Input
parameters.description: useinput - Uses the input's resolution. eighth - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. quarter - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. half - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 2x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 4x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. 8x - Multiply the input's resolution by that amount. fit - Fits the width and height to the resolution given below, while maintaining the aspect ratio. limit - The width and height are limited to the resolution given below. If one of the dimensions exceeds the given resolution, the width and height will be reduced to fit inside the given limits while maintaining the aspect ratio. custom - Enables the  parameter below, giving direct control over width and height.

parameters.name: Resolution
parameters.label: Resolution
parameters.description:
resolution -  - Enabled only when the  parameter is set to Custom . Some Generators like Constant and Ramp do not use inputs and only use this field to determine their size. The drop down menu on the right provides some commonly used resolutions.

 resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: W
parameters.label: W
parameters.description: resolutionw - resolutionh -

parameters.name: Resolution Menu
parameters.label: Resolution Menu
parameters.description: resmenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used resolutions.

parameters.name: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.label: Use Global Res Multiplier
parameters.description: resmult - Uses the Global  Multiplier found in Edit>Preferences>TOPs. This multiplies all the TOPs resolutions by the set amount. This is handy when working on computers with different hardware specifications. If a project is designed on a desktop workstation with lots of graphics memory, a user on a laptop with only 64MB VRAM can set the Global  Multiplier to a value of half or quarter so it runs at an acceptable speed. By checking this checkbox on, this  is affected by the global multiplier.

parameters.name: Output Aspect
parameters.label: Output Aspect
parameters.description:
outputaspect -  - Sets the image aspect ratio allowing any textures to be viewed in any size. Watch for unexpected results when compositing TOPs with different aspect ratios. (You can define images with non-square pixels using xres, yres, aspectx, aspecty where xres/yres != aspectx/aspecty.)

 useinput - Uses the input's aspect ratio. resolution - Uses the aspect of the image's defined resolution (ie 512x256 would be 2:1), whereby each pixel is square. custom - Lets you explicitly define a custom aspect ratio in the Aspect parameter below.

parameters.name: Aspect
parameters.label: Aspect
parameters.description:
aspect -  - Use when Output Aspect parameter is set to Custom Aspect.

 aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect1
parameters.label: Aspect1
parameters.description: aspect1 - aspect2 -

parameters.name: Aspect Menu
parameters.label: Aspect Menu
parameters.description: armenu - A drop-down menu with some commonly used aspect ratios.

parameters.name: Input Smoothness
parameters.label: Input Smoothness
parameters.description:
inputfiltertype -  - This controls pixel filtering on the input image of the .

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Nearest Pixel
parameters.label: Nearest Pixel
parameters.description: nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. This is how you get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Fill Viewer
parameters.label: Fill Viewer
parameters.description:
fillmode -  - Determine how the  image is displayed in the viewer.
NOTE:To get an understanding of how TOPs work with images, you will want to set this to Native  as you lay down TOPs when starting out. This will let you see what is actually happening without any automatic viewer resizing.


 useinput - Uses the same Fill Viewer settings as it's input. fill - Stretches the image to fit the edges of the viewer. width - Stretches image to fit viewer horizontally. height - Stretches image to fit viewer vertically. best - Stretches or squashes image so no part of image is cropped. outside - Stretches or squashes image so image fills viewer while constraining it's proportions. This often leads to part of image getting cropped by viewer. nativeres - Displays the native resolution of the image in the viewer.

parameters.name: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.label: Viewer Smoothness
parameters.description:
filtertype -  - This controls pixel filtering in the viewers.

 nearest - Uses nearest pixel or accurate image representation. Images will look jaggy when viewing at any zoom level other than Native . linear - Uses linear filtering between pixels. Use this to get  images in viewers to look good at various zoom levels, especially useful when using any Fill Viewer setting other than Native . mipmap - Uses  mipmap filtering when scaling images. This can be used to reduce artifacts and sparkling in moving/scaling images that have lots of detail.

parameters.name: Passes
parameters.label: Passes
parameters.description: npasses - Duplicates the operation of the  the specified number of times. Making this larger than 1 is essentially the same as taking the output from each pass, and passing it into the first input of the node and repeating the process. Other inputs and parameters remain the same for each pass.

parameters.name: Channel Mask
parameters.label: Channel Mask
parameters.description: chanmask - Allows you to choose which channels (R, G, B, or A) the  will operate on. All channels are selected by default.

parameters.name: Pixel Format
parameters.label: Pixel Format
parameters.description: Pixel Format parameter.
